name: NAPALM (Network Automation and Programmability Abstraction Layer with Multivendor support)

auth.notes: SSH/API credentials for each network device

performance.notes: Network device interaction latency; varies by device and operation

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-vendor network device abstraction. get_driver('ios'), get_driver('eos'), get_driver('junos'), get_driver('nxos'). Uniform API: device.get_facts(), get_interfaces(), get_bgp_neighbors(), get_route_to(). Config management: load_merge_candidate(), commit_config(), rollback(). Cisco IOS/NX-OS, Juniper JunOS, Arista EOS, Fortinet, VyOS. Used with Nornir, Ansible, Netbox. Abstracts vendor CLI/API differences. Used by network engineers for automation.

gotchas: Driver quality varies by vendor (EOS best, some drivers less maintained)
Requires device access and SSH/API credentials
Some features not available on all platforms
Not async-native (use in threading or with Nornir)
best_when: Multi-vendor network automation needing single Python API
Pairing with Nornir for parallel multi-device operations
summary: NAPALM abstracts multi-vendor network device operations (Cisco IOS/NXOS, Juniper, Arista, Fortinet) into a uniform Python API. get_facts(), get_interfaces(), get_bgp_neighbors(). Config management with commit/rollback. Used with Nornir for parallel multi-device automation. The standard for multi-vendor NetDevOps automation.
